pat_note_resolved_by_operator: 2026-04-25 (CORRECTED, supersedes 2026-04-23 entry): The 'update CLAUDE.md PAT' TODO was NOT a phantom. Project CLAUDE.md (md5 7caa81c513c47df50e47a5ee3ffc4903) is clean — that part of the 04-23 investigation was correct. But user-level CLAUDE.md (~/CLAUDE.md or ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md, outside operator session visibility) did contain a Devilwench KEV-scoped PAT that the decider correctly identified each run. Operator confirmed Devilwench PAT was already expired/invalid and removed it from user-level CLAUDE.md on 2026-04-25. The earlier HNOTE-DECIDER-CLAUDE-MD-PAT-PHANTOM-001 has been superseded by HNOTE-DECIDER-CLAUDE-MD-PAT-CORRECTED-002 with the corrected story. Decider agents: do not re-flag this; source is removed.
notes_this_run: Mode 1 — EXP-267 written, addressing SEC-4.2.2.c2 H1 (no-physical-mechanism misattribution) and H2 (Sq-residual fourth horn). last_analyst_note:
Mode 1 (Section Expansion) @ 2026-04-27T07:12:19.170Z.

DISPATCHER TRACE (against fresh clone refreshed at PRELUDE from origin/main):
  Mode 0 — WIN onboarding: workspace=71, GitHub=71. NO WORK.
  Mode 1 — Expansion queue: 1 actionable pending (EXP-267, high priority). FIRED.
  (Modes 1b/2/2b/3/4 not evaluated; Mode 1 has priority and produces work.)
  Assigned-analyst issues: 3 — ISS-1578 (minor, EXP-254 §4.2.7 stale row, deferred); ISS-1602 + ISS-1603 (both directly resolved by EXP-267 below).

WORK PERFORMED (Mode 1 single-EXP run):
  Wrote monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-267.json — single tight EXP for SEC-4.2.2.c2 H1+H2 follow-up patches per c2 reviewer's recommendation.

  H1 (MAJOR — 'no physical mechanism' misattribution):
    Verified live on 2026-04-27: curl of https://john09289.github.io/predictions/predictions.html confirms Tier 1 magnetic block (line 113 of fetched HTML) reads verbatim:
      "Globe prediction: 0.0 nT systematic (or ionospheric Sq disruption — competing mechanism exists, see WIN-068 honest caveat)."
    Confirmed (via grep over fetched HTML) that 'no physical mechanism' does not appear in dome material at all. The 'no mechanism' / 'cannot produce' phrasings on the dome's site apply to gravity Tier 2 ('ionospheric mechanism cannot produce ground-level g changes') and Tier 3 ('no mechanism'), NOT magnetic Tier 1.
    EXP-267 paragraph 5 replaced: removed misattribution, quoted dome's Tier 1 verbatim, pivoted to footnote-vs-operational-rule structural critique. Preserved 'refined for over ninety years' beat by folding into new paragraph.

  H2 (MODERATE — Sq-residual fourth horn):
    Verified live on 2026-04-27: curl of https://john09289.github.io/predictions/live.html confirms Domain 11 falsification rule reads byte-identical to c2 capture: "Eclipse 2026 — Fails if measured anomaly is within ±3 nT of 0 (globe prediction)."
    EXP-267 added new paragraph after closing trilemma engaging post-Sq-residual defense: dome owes pre-registered Sq-subtraction methodology anchored before measurement (otherwise moveable goalpost); on the only reading the dome has actually anchored (raw measurement vs 0 nT), the BOU-2017 trilemma still fires.

  MINOR / polish (c2 4(a)):
    Hoisted §4.2.7 forward-link parenthetical to its own dedicated paragraph at the end of the section, so trilemma → fourth-horn → §4.2.7 link each own a closing beat. Resolves c2's rhetorical-dilution concern.

  TLDR (c2 minor 'sits outside' phrasing):
    LEFT UNCHANGED. c2 explicitly marked optional and not blocking. Body's reconstructed closing argument is more robust against post-Sq-residual escape (the readier defender line) than against TLDR-category-error. Editing TLDR risks introducing fresh micro-error for marginal gain.

  c1 stronger_arguments[0] (E001-E009 self-falsification on dome's own forecast):
    NOT incorporated (was missed-strengthening, not a hole). Decider can spawn a follow-up ISS for one-sentence add if appetite remains; not blocking.

DRIFT-CHECK CONFIRMATION:
  predictions.html md5=885cd58727130018764b14355b0aa785 (matches poller's 14-day byte-identical streak captured in last_poll_note 2026-04-26T23:06:00Z).
  live.html Domain-11 rule byte-identical to c2 capture (2026-04-27T03:37:21.925Z).

USER-PREFERENCE DIRECTIVE ('Think about the other side too') — captured in EXP-267 anticipated_objections and user_preference_directive_capture:
  Defender retreat (a): 'WIN-068 honest_caveat IS the methodology' → countered: footnote acknowledgement ≠ pre-registered methodology (no specified Sq baseline / window / station-set / aggregation).
  Defender retreat (b): 'rule applies to residual' → countered by the new fourth-horn paragraph (no published methodology, moveable goalpost).
  Defender retreat (c): 'we'll publish a Sq-subtraction methodology now' → countered: any post-hoc methodology must be OTS-anchored before August 12, 2026 to be pre-registered with respect to the measurement; if so anchored, that's the canary fix and our review updates to engage the published methodology on its merits; if unanchored, moveable-goalpost critique fires harder.
  None of these defender retreats survives without a methodological commitment that, made properly, would itself constitute the canary-detected fix.

AUTHOR-CONTACT PRIOR (per HNOTE-OPERATOR-AUTHOR-CONTACT-CONFIRMED-001 consumed prior run): EXP-267 verdicts and arguments are derived strictly from publicly-visible dome content (verified curl of predictions.html and live.html). No reliance on operator's private DM evidence. Verdict-level (Refuted by Data) unchanged.

ISSUE-RESOLUTION SIGNALS WRITTEN (for decider to act on):
  monitor/analyst/issue-proposals/proposal-ISS-1602-resolution.json — recommends close once EXP-267 integrates
  monitor/analyst/issue-proposals/proposal-ISS-1603-resolution.json — recommends close once EXP-267 integrates

PROCESS NOTE (self-correction):
  Initial writes this run landed in the transient clone at PRELUDE; the cleanup step (rm -rf clone) deleted them before they were copied to FUSE. Self-corrected before exit by reconstructing all 5 outputs (EXP-267.json, 2x proposals, expansion-tracker.json, this status.json) directly into the FUSE workspace. Prior-run convention (e.g., 2026-04-27T05:10:48Z Mode 2b note) is to write FUSE-canonical files (attention-inbox.json) directly to FUSE; the analyst.md "write outputs to workspace" directive applies to all outputs, not only the FUSE-canonical ones, since universal-pusher rescues from FUSE→git on its 4h cycle. Tinker note for next run (below).

NOT DONE (intentional):
  Did NOT git commit/push (analyst.md forbidden — decider integrates EXP-267 into data/sections.json).
  Did NOT run tests/run.sh (no data file edits this run; only EXP/proposals/tracker writes; CLAUDE.md test rule applies to docs/*.html DATA blob edits).
  Did NOT touch ISS-1578 (minor stale row reference, still deferred — pickup if no higher-priority work next run; multiple prior runs have deferred this so it may need decider attention if it stays parked).
  Did NOT process Mode 2b (5 pending in FUSE — same backlog as prior run; Mode 1 had higher priority per dispatcher).
  Did NOT process changes_pending_analysis=2 (still carrying — Mode 1 has priority over normal analysis when an actionable expansion exists).

NEXT-RUN GUIDANCE for analyst:
  Mode 0 likely NO WORK (count=71/71 expected to hold).
  Mode 1: NO WORK after this — only EXP-267 was actionable.
  Mode 1b: ALL DONE (verified this run).
  Mode 2: NO NOTES (verified this run; HNOTE-OPERATOR-AUTHOR-CONTACT-CONFIRMED-001 already consumed prior run).
  Mode 2b: 5 pending in FUSE — drain candidate next run (4 OBE-eligible per prior analyst note; 1 substantive singleton ATT-2026-04-23T02:31:11.720Z for WIN-061 EXP-232 content verification).
  Mode 3: NO PENDING DEFENSES.
  Mode 4: ALL DONE.
  Assigned-issues: ISS-1578 still parked (minor); after decider integrates EXP-267, ISS-1602 and ISS-1603 will close.
  changes_pending_analysis=2 — still carrying; will be next-run normal-analysis target if no higher-priority mode triggers.

NEXT-RUN GUIDANCE for decider:
  Integrate EXP-267 (replacement_html replaces full §4.2.2 / id=p4-422 section). 671 words replacement vs 567 original (+104 net). Net structural changes: paragraph-5 rewrite, new fourth-horn paragraph after trilemma, §4.2.7 forward-link hoisted to own paragraph.
  After integration: close ISS-1602 (H1 resolved), close ISS-1603 (H2 resolved); both proposals already in monitor/analyst/issue-proposals/.
  Optional: spawn one-sentence ISS for c1 stronger_arguments[0] (E001-E009 self-falsification add). Not required.
  Sync expansion-tracker EXP-227/EXP-232 status (flagged in prior runs — still showing 'ready-for-integration' though content is integrated in canonical wins.json).

NEXT-RUN GUIDANCE for curmudgeon:
  After EXP-267 integration, c3 review of §4.2.2 may be appropriate (the paragraph-5 rewrite + new fourth-horn paragraph are substantive new prose surface that hasn't been adversarially reviewed). Not high priority — c2 verdict held; the patches address known holes; c3 would be confirming the patches don't introduce new holes.

NEXT-RUN GUIDANCE for tinker:
  Pipeline observation from this run: the analyst.md instructions are ambiguous about WHERE to write expansion outputs. Step 0a creates a clone for shared-writer reads; the expansion procedure says "Write the replacement text to monitor/analyst/expansions/{item-id}.json" (relative path); the global rule says "Write outputs to workspace. The decider handles git." The unambiguous read of "workspace" is the FUSE workspace mount, not the transient clone. This run discovered the gap when its initial clone-side writes were deleted at cleanup; self-corrected by reconstructing into FUSE.
    Tinker recommendation: codify in analyst.md that all output writes (EXP-NNN.json, proposal-*.json, expansion-tracker.json updates, status.json updates) must use the FUSE WORKSPACE path explicitly, mirroring Mode 2b's existing "/sessions/hopeful-wonderful-albattani/mnt/dome-model-review/monitor/analyst/attention-inbox.json" guidance. The clone is read-only for analyst purposes (PRELUDE refresh provides fresh shared-writer state for analysis; writes go to FUSE; workspace-sync universal-pusher then pushes FUSE→git on its 4h cycle).
    Prior-run notes still standing (no new resolution): (1) Mode 2b OBE check should add issue.fixed_at fallback; (2) expansion-tracker EXP-227/EXP-232 status drift; (3) FUSE-vs-clone attention-inbox.json divergence.

NEXT-RUN GUIDANCE for poller:
  No change — continue monitoring. Watch for: (1) any dome-side Sq-subtraction methodology document (would be a structural response to §4.2.2 patch; classify likely-responsive per author-contact prior); (2) any dome edit to predictions.html Tier 1 magnetic block softening or strengthening the WIN-068 honest_caveat language; (3) PRED-105 and PRED-106 closure handling per existing watch list.

NEXT-RUN GUIDANCE for social / integrity / workspace-sync:
  Workspace-sync: pick up EXP-267 + 2 proposals + tracker update on next 4h run (universal-pusher per CLAUDE.md).
  Integrity: §4.2.2 will have churn after decider integrates; cross-page consistency check on next 9 AM run.
  Social: no immediate impact; section-level rewrite, no new claim beat-worthy.

prev_analyst_run.note:
Mode 2 (Human Note consumption) @ 2026-04-27T06:03:36.000Z. DISPATCHER TRACE (against fresh clone refreshed at PRELUDE from origin/main commit 2741eb9): Mode 0 — WIN onboarding: workspace=71, GitHub=71. NO WORK. Mode 1 — Expansion queue: NO ACTIONABLE EXPANSIONS. Mode 1b — Prediction writeups: ALL DONE. Mode 2 — Human notes: 1 pending (HNOTE-OPERATOR-AUTHOR-CONTACT-CONFIRMED-001). FIRED. (Modes 2b/3/4 not evaluated; Mode 2 has priority over Mode 2b and produces work.) Assigned-analyst issues: 1 (ISS-1578, minor). DEFERRED. WORK PERFORMED: Consumed HNOTE-OPERATOR-AUTHOR-CONTACT-CONFIRMED-001 — operator's 2026-04-27 update that the dome author has made direct DM contact with the operator (Sunday 2026-04-26 evening), confirmed-genuine via content match (messages referenced Tesla 11.79 Hz, piezoelectric measurements, eclipse magnetic predictions — material this review covers). Note routes to analyst with priority=medium and explicitly states "no specific action requested. Just internalize the new prior." CLAUDE.md "Author Contact Status" section verified present (line 24, both clone and FUSE) — the canonical statement of the new prior is in CLAUDE.md and this HNOTE is the ensure-you-see-it-immediately mechanism. NEW PRIOR INTERNALIZED: - Dome-site change with timing+content alignment to a recent edit/emphasis on our side → classify likely-responsive (high confidence), no longer needs the caveat that "we don't know whether the author has seen our review." - Dome-site change on a topic that has been static for weeks, immediately after our public emphasis on that topic → likely-responsive. - Random churn (typo fixes, automated commits, unrelated WIN edits) remains random; the prior shifts but doesn't make everything responsive. DISCIPLINE THAT DOES NOT CHANGE (re-affirmed): - Reason from publicly-visible dome-site changes only (poller change reports, dome HTML diffs). Operator-side direct evidence (DMs) is NOT in the review sources and MUST NOT be used to assert "this is responsive" — assertions still rest on dome's published surface (timing + content alignment). - Verdicts remain empirical/scientific against the published model. Author-contact informs *severity and classification* of dome-side moves, NOT the validity of any specific scientific argument. - The OTS canary still stands as the explicit fixed-prediction structural signal. Direct DM contact is a parallel confirmation channel, not a replacement; if/when the dome author separates predictions from observations into independently-anchored OTS files, that remains the strong fix-in-response indicator. DUAL-WRITE COMPLETED (per CLAUDE.md "Human Notes Rule"): - Updated /sessions/admiring-adoring-volta/mnt/dome-model-review/monitor/analyst/human-notes.json (FUSE) — pending=0 - Updated /sessions/admiring-adoring-volta/dome-review-clean/monitor/analyst/human-notes.json (clone) — pending=0 - Both files JSON-validated post-write. - Note now has status="consumed", consumed_at, consumed_by (821-char internalization summary). USER-PREFERENCE DIRECTIVE ("Think about the other side of the argument too") — captured here: Counter-argument: a defender could argue that the prior shouldn't shift at all on the basis of operator's private DM evidence — it could be a phishing impersonation, a LARP, or content-match could be reverse-engineered from our public site. Counter-counter: the operator confirmed content match against material that goes beyond what's on our published surface (the specific pairing of Tesla 11.79 Hz + piezoelectric + eclipse magnetic predictions in a single conversational thread is consistent with someone who actually authored the dome model, not someone who skimmed our review); the discipline guardrails ensure we don't *cite* operator's private evidence in any review surface, so even if the DM were spoofed, our published review remains unaffected — only our *internal classification prior* shifts. The downside risk is contained. A second defender argument: an analyst doesn't NEED to consume informational HNOTEs — they could just read CLAUDE.md and continue. Counter: the procedure (analyst-mode1-expansions.md) explicitly says "After incorporating: set note status to 'consumed', add consumed_at timestamp and consumed_by note." Even informational notes get consumed once internalized, otherwise the pending-detection in Mode 2 dispatcher would trip on the same note every run forever. NOT DONE (intentional): Did NOT git commit/push (analyst.md forbidden). Workspace-sync handles human-notes.json explicitly per CLAUDE.md note ("workspace-sync only copies monitor/analyst/human-notes.json explicitly") — universal-pusher will push the FUSE→git copy on its next 4h run. Did NOT process Mode 2b (5 pending in FUSE / 70 in clone — known divergence flagged in prior run), Mode 3 (no surviving defenses), Mode 4 (idle), or normal analysis (changes_pending_analysis=2 still carrying). Per dispatcher rule "All other modes: write summary and stop" — Mode 2 consumed this run. Did NOT process assigned issue ISS-1578 (minor, EXP-254 §4.2.7 stale row reference) — Mode 2 had priority. Did NOT write an expansion or proposal — note explicitly stated no action required. NEXT-RUN GUIDANCE for analyst: Mode 0/1/1b/3/4 likely all NO WORK (per current state). Mode 2 should be 0 pending after this consume. Mode 2b: 5 pending in FUSE (vs 70 in clone — divergence persists). Recommend continuing three-phase drain in next run; tinker is already aware of the workspace-sync attention-inbox.json divergence (flagged 2026-04-27T04:14:57Z prior analyst run). Assigned issues: ISS-1578 (minor) — pickup if no higher-priority work. changes_pending_analysis=2 still carrying. NEXT-RUN GUIDANCE for decider: No new EXP from this run. EXP-266 (prior analyst run, WIN-044 typo "stency.

" → ".

") still ready for integration, low priority. NEXT-RUN GUIDANCE for tinker: No new pipeline issues from this run. Prior FUSE-vs-clone attention-inbox divergence still standing (flagged earlier today). The fact that this analyst run found the HNOTE in both places (clone and FUSE) and was able to dual-write cleanly suggests human-notes.json sync is functioning — the divergence is specific to attention-inbox.json. NEXT-RUN GUIDANCE for poller: Update prior on dome-site change classification. When you observe a dome change that aligns in timing and content with one of our recent edits, you may now report it to the analyst as "likely-responsive" with higher confidence (the author-has-seen-this-review uncertainty has been resolved on the operator side). Continue surfacing the timing+content evidence in your change reports — analyst still classifies from that evidence, not from operator-private knowledge. NEXT-RUN GUIDANCE for curmudgeon / social / integrity / workspace-sync: No direct impact this run. The author-contact prior is not a verdict-affecting fact; verdicts remain empirical against the published model.
